content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 26, 1977

Ms. J. Wiegand
City Attorney
City Hall
100 Valley Blvd.
Escondido, California 92025

Dear Ms. Wiegand:

Thank you for your telegram of May 26, 1977, in which you
seek advice regarding the responsibilities of City Councilman
Jerry Harmon under the Political Reform Act. You ask whether,
pursuant to Government Code §87100 and the disqualification
provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the City of
Escondido, Councilman Harmon may participate in the determination
to establish a redevelopment agency within the city. This
response to your inquiry constitutes staff advice pursuant to
Government Code §8314(b).

You relate that the City of Escondido is in the process of
selecting a site for a regional shopping center. One proposed
site would involve the creation of a redevelopment agency. One
parcel which would be compelled to be condemned upon the establish-
ment of the redevelopment agency has been leased by the Pacific
Telephone Company under a 20-year leasehold currently in effect.
The rent payable over the term of the lease amounts to more than
$654,000.00. Councilman Harmon is an engineering manager for
the Pacific Telephone Company. Thus, the company is a source
of income to the Councilman and the Councilman holds a position
of management with the company. [See, Government Code §§87103(c)
and (d)].

The issue presented by the facts, above, is whether the
Councilman may vote or otherwise participate in the determina-
tion to establish a redevelopment agency in the City of Escondido.
Government Code §§87100 and 87103, as well as the disqualifi-
cation provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the City
of Escondido, provide that the Councilman may not make or parti-
cipate in the making of a decision when it is reasonably foresee-
able that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on one
of his interests, in this case, the Pacific Telephone Company.

Ms. J. Wiegand
May 26, 1977
page two


     It is concluded, based upon the facts recited, above,
and in your telegram, that Mr. Harmon may participate in the
determination to establish the redevelopment agency. The
company will receive only the fair market value of its lease-
hold interest in the property to be condemned. Thus, it is
not foreseeable that any of the threshold monetary limits estab-
lished in the Commission's guidelines at 2 Cal. Adm. Code
§18702(b)(1)&(3) would be approached in the condemnation which
would follow the establishment of the agency. Furthermore,
under the facts as presented and in light of all of the circum-
stances, there appears to be no reason to believe that the
existence of the Councilman's interest in the condemnation would
interfere with the Councilman's performance of his duties in
an impartial manner free from bias. [See, 2 Cal. Adm. Code
§18702(a)].

     This advice is, of course, limited to the determination
to establish the redevelopment agency and the resultant conse-
quences thereof. In the event that legal or administrative
challenge is instituted by the condemnee, we advise that you
seek further advice from the Fair Political Practices Commission.

     If I may be of further assistance to you in this matter,
please feel free to call me at (916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mfa

sections.question: Whether, pursuant to Government Code §87100 and the disqualification provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the City of Escondido, Councilman Harmon may participate in the determination to establish a redevelopment agency within the city.
sections.conclusion: It is concluded, based upon the facts recited, above, and in your telegram, that Mr. Harmon may participate in the determination to establish the redevelopment agency. The company will receive only the fair market value of its leasehold interest in the property to be condemned. Thus, it is not foreseeable that any of the threshold monetary limits established in the Commission's guidelines at 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18702(b)(1)&(3) would be approached in the condemnation which would follow the establishment of the agency. Furthermore, under the facts as presented and in light of all of the circumstances, there appears to be no reason to believe that the existence of the Councilman's interest in the condemnee would interfere with the Councilman's performance of his duties in an impartial manner free from bias.
sections.question_synthetic: May City Councilman Jerry Harmon, who is an engineering manager for Pacific Telephone Company, participate in voting on the establishment of a redevelopment agency that would result in the condemnation of property leased by his employer?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Councilman Harmon may participate in the redevelopment agency determination because the company will receive only fair market value compensation, the financial impact would not meet the Commission's threshold limits, and there is no evidence his impartiality would be compromised.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document ID 77ADV-77-247 from 1977. Signed by Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief of Conflicts of Interest Division. Includes specific Government Code citations and administrative code references.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether, pursuant to Government Code §87100 and the disqualification provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the City of Escondido, Councilman Harmon may participate in the determination to establish a redevelopment agency within the city.

CONCLUSION: It is concluded, based upon the facts recited, above, and in your telegram, that Mr. Harmon may participate in the determination to establish the redevelopment agency. The company will receive only the fair market value of its leasehold interest in the property to be condemned. Thus, it is not foreseeable that any of the threshold monetary limits established in the Commission's guidelines at 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18702(b)(1)&(3) would be approached in the condemnation which would follow the establishment of the agency. Furthermore, under the facts as presented and in light of all of the circumstances, there appears to be no reason to believe that the existence of the Councilman's interest in the condemnee would interfere with the Councilman's performance of his duties in an impartial manner free from bias.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the City Attorney of Escondido that Councilman Jerry Harmon, an engineering manager for Pacific Telephone Company, may participate in voting to establish a redevelopment agency that would result in condemnation of property leased by his employer, since the company would receive fair market value and the financial impact falls below conflict of interest thresholds.
